title: Switzerland Votes on Controversial Plan to Limit Population
subtitle: Voters decide whether to cap the nation's population at 10 million to manage growth and immigration.

summary.en: Switzerland is holding a national vote on a proposal to limit its total population to 10 million people. The plan, supported by the Swiss People’s Party, aims to address concerns about housing, schools, and transport. Opponents, including the government and business leaders, warn that the move could damage the economy and threaten national stability. If passed, the government would have to restrict immigration and potentially end key agreements with the European Union. The outcome of this referendum will have significant effects on the country’s future and its relationship with its neighbors.
summary.zh: 瑞士正在舉行一場全國公投，提案將總人口限制在一千萬人以內。該計畫由瑞士人民黨支持，旨在解決住房、學校與交通方面的擔憂。包括政府與企業領袖在內的反對者則警告，此舉可能損害經濟並威脅國家穩定。若提案通過，政府將必須限制移民，並可能終止與歐盟的關鍵協議。這場公投的結果將對瑞士的未來及其與鄰國的關係產生重大影響。

paragraphs.en: Switzerland is currently waiting for the results of a historic national vote that could change the country’s future. Citizens are deciding whether to support a proposal that would limit the total population of the nation to 10 million people. This vote is part of the Swiss system of direct democracy, where citizens can propose changes to the law if they gather enough signatures. This specific initiative was organized by the Swiss People’s Party (SVP), which has long argued that the country is growing too fast.
paragraphs.zh: 瑞士目前正等待一場歷史性全國公投的結果，這場投票可能會改變國家的未來。公民們正在決定是否支持一項將全國總人口限制在一千萬人的提案。這場投票是瑞士直接民主制度的一部分，只要公民收集到足夠的連署，就能提出法律變更。這項具體的倡議是由瑞士人民黨（Swiss People’s Party, SVP）所發起，該黨長期以來一直主張國家人口成長過快。

paragraphs.en: The proposal is significant because it suggests a strict limit. If the population reaches 9.5 million, the government would be required to enforce tough new rules on residency permits, family reunions, and asylum requests. If the population continues to grow and passes the 10 million mark before 2050, the government would be forced to end its free movement agreement with the European Union. This agreement currently allows people from EU countries to live and work in Switzerland easily. Ending it would mean Switzerland would lose its access to the European single market, which is a vital part of the Swiss economy.
paragraphs.zh: 這項提案之所以重要，是因為它提出了一個嚴格的上限。如果人口達到九百五十萬，政府將被要求對居留許可、家庭團聚及庇護申請執行嚴格的新規定。如果人口持續成長並在二〇五〇年前突破一千萬大關，政府將被迫終止與歐盟的自由移動協議。該協議目前允許歐盟國家的人民輕鬆地在瑞士居住與工作。終止此協議將意味著瑞士失去進入歐洲單一市場的管道，而這正是瑞士經濟至關重要的一部分。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the SVP argues that the current level of immigration is causing problems for everyday life. Supporters of the plan say that the arrival of many new residents puts too much pressure on housing, schools, and transportation systems. The party claims that while they are not against all immigration, they want it to be more moderate and controlled. Thomas Matter, an MP from the SVP, told The Guardian that the country has moved from a system of "qualitative immigration"—where specific skills are prioritized—to a system of "quantitative immigration," which he believes is unsustainable.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》報導，瑞士人民黨主張目前的移民水準正對日常生活造成問題。該計畫的支持者表示，大量新居民的到來對住房、學校及交通系統造成了過大的壓力。該黨聲稱，他們並非反對所有移民，而是希望移民能更溫和且受到控制。瑞士人民黨國會議員馬特（Thomas Matter）向《衛報》表示，國家已經從「品質移民」制度（優先考慮特定技能）轉變為「數量移民」制度，他認為這種做法無法持續。

paragraphs.en: However, the proposal faces strong opposition. The seven-member Swiss government, which includes ministers from the four largest political parties, has officially recommended that voters reject the plan. They warn that such a move would hurt the country’s prosperity and damage its national stability. Many other groups, including the Swiss trade union federation and the Swiss Employers’ Association, agree with the government. They argue that the plan is a simple solution to a very complex problem.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這項提案面臨強烈反對。由四大政黨部長組成的瑞士七人聯邦委員會，已正式建議選民否決該計畫。他們警告，此舉將損害國家的繁榮並破壞國家穩定。包括瑞士工會聯盟與瑞士雇主協會等許多團體，也都同意政府的看法。他們認為，這項計畫只是針對複雜問題所提出的簡化解決方案。

paragraphs.en: Rudolf Minsch, the chief economist at the business group Economiesuisse, told The Guardian that the proposal offers an "illusion of a free lunch." He believes that capping the population will not actually solve issues like high housing costs or traffic congestion. Instead, he and other experts argue that the country needs workers from abroad. Like many other European nations, Switzerland has a low birth rate and an aging population. Government data shows that the number of people over the age of 65 is expected to grow significantly by 2055. Without new workers coming into the country, it will be difficult to support the needs of an older society.
paragraphs.zh: 商業團體「瑞士經濟」（Economiesuisse）的首席經濟學家明施（Rudolf Minsch）向《衛報》表示，該提案提供了一種「免費午餐的錯覺」。他認為，限制人口上限並不能真正解決高房價或交通擁擠等問題。相反地，他與其他專家主張，國家需要來自海外的勞動力。如同許多其他歐洲國家，瑞士面臨低出生率與人口老化的問題。政府數據顯示，六十五歲以上人口預計在二〇五五年將顯著成長。若沒有新勞動力進入，將難以支撐高齡社會的需求。

paragraphs.en: This debate highlights a growing tension in Europe. Many populist right-wing parties have used similar arguments about immigration to gain support, similar to the discussions seen during the Brexit vote in Britain or the rise of political groups in France and Germany. Yet, Switzerland is unique in this situation. Philippe Wanner, a demography expert at the University of Geneva, noted in The Guardian that while many countries try to limit immigration, no other nation has ever held a vote to put a hard cap on its total population.
paragraphs.zh: 這場辯論凸顯了歐洲日益緊張的局勢。許多民粹主義右翼政黨都曾利用類似的移民論點來爭取支持，這與英國脫歐公投期間或法國與德國政治團體興起時的討論如出一轍。然而，瑞士的情況相當獨特。日內瓦大學的人口學專家瓦納（Philippe Wanner）在《衛報》中指出，雖然許多國家試圖限制移民，但從未有其他國家舉行過公投來為總人口設定硬性上限。

paragraphs.en: Since the referendum was announced in February, the discussion has been intense. While the SVP has been a powerful force in Swiss politics for decades, recent opinion polls suggest that the campaign against the proposal has gained support. The outcome of this vote will be watched closely by other nations, as it represents a major test of how a wealthy, modern country balances its economic needs with public concerns about rapid social change. For now, the Swiss people must decide if they want to keep their current open system or take a new, more restrictive path.
paragraphs.zh: 自從二月宣布舉行公投以來，討論一直非常激烈。儘管瑞士人民黨數十年來一直是瑞士政壇的一股強大力量，但近期的民調顯示，反對該提案的運動已獲得支持。這場投票的結果將受到其他國家的密切關注，因為它代表了一個富裕的現代國家如何在經濟需求與公眾對快速社會變遷的擔憂之間取得平衡的重大考驗。目前，瑞士人民必須決定是要維持現行的開放制度，還是要採取一條全新的、更具限制性的道路。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that is obtained without any effort or cost, often used to describe an unrealistic expectation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 免費午餐（比喻不勞而獲或不切實際的幻想）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處並非指真的午餐，而是比喻一種不切實際的解決方案。文中：Rudolf Minsch, the chief economist at the business group Economiesuisse, told The Guardian that the proposal offers an "illusion of a free lunch."

keyTerms.definitionEn: A strict, fixed upper limit that cannot be exceeded.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 硬性上限（嚴格規定的最高限額）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡的 hard 指的是「嚴格的、不可變通的」，而非「困難的」。文中：Philippe Wanner, a demography expert at the University of Geneva, noted in The Guardian that while many countries try to limit immigration, no other nation has ever held a vote to put a hard cap on its total population.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A burden or strain caused by excessive demand or difficulty.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 負擔、壓力（指對資源造成的負荷）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於情緒壓力，這裡指對公共設施造成的負荷。文中：Supporters of the plan say that the arrival of many new residents puts too much pressure on housing, schools, and transportation systems.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Swiss wait to hear result of ballot on capping population at 10 million

quiz.question: What specific action is the government required to take if the Swiss population reaches 9.5 million?

options.A: They must immediately end the free movement agreement with the EU.
options.B: They must implement stricter regulations regarding residency permits and family reunions.
options.C: They must stop all immigration from countries outside of Europe.
options.D: They must hold a new national vote to decide on future economic policies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，當人口達到 950 萬時，政府必須針對居留許可、家庭團聚及庇護申請執行嚴格的新規定。選項 A 錯誤，因為終止歐盟自由移動協議是在人口達到 1000 萬時才會發生的強制措施；選項 C 文章未提及；選項 D 則與文章提到的直接民主程序無關。

quiz.question: Based on the arguments presented, why might the Swiss business community be particularly worried about the proposed population cap?

options.A: They fear that a smaller population will lead to an increase in the birth rate.
options.B: They believe that the cap will make it easier to manage housing and transportation.
options.C: They are concerned that a shrinking workforce will be unable to support the aging population.
options.D: They think that the government will lose its ability to collect taxes from foreign workers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第五段提到瑞士面臨低出生率與人口老化問題，專家指出若沒有外來勞動力，將難以支撐高齡化社會的需求。因此，企業界擔心限制人口會導致勞動力短缺，進而影響國家經濟。選項 A 與事實相反；選項 B 是支持者的看法，而非企業界的擔憂；選項 D 並非文中提到的核心經濟問題。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does Rudolf Minsch mean by the phrase 'illusion of a free lunch'?

options.A: A proposal that promises benefits without acknowledging the necessary costs or negative side effects.
options.B: A government policy that provides free food and services to new immigrants.
options.C: A mistaken belief that the economy will grow automatically without any effort.
options.D: A plan that is too expensive for the government to implement effectively.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。「Free lunch」在經濟學隱喻中指「天下沒有白吃的午餐」，意指獲得好處卻不需付出代價。Minsch 使用此詞是為了批評該提案看似能解決住房或交通問題，實則忽略了限制人口帶來的經濟負面代價與現實困難。選項 B 是字面上的誤解；選項 C 與 D 雖與經濟相關，但未精確捕捉該片語在文中「看似美好卻不切實際」的語境。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central conflict described in the article?

options.A: The Swiss government is trying to force citizens to accept more immigrants to boost the economy.
options.B: The Swiss People’s Party is attempting to replace the current government through a national referendum.
options.C: Switzerland is debating whether to prioritize national population control over its economic integration and labor needs.
options.D: The European Union is threatening to cut ties with Switzerland if they do not change their immigration laws.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文核心在於探討瑞士一項具爭議的公投，該公投試圖在「限制人口成長」與「維持經濟繁榮及勞動力需求」之間做出抉擇。選項 A 錯誤，政府是反對該提案的；選項 B 誇大了該黨的目的；選項 D 則是因果倒置，是瑞士的提案可能導致與歐盟協議終止，而非歐盟主動發出威脅。
